title: Music & Concerts

vocab.genre: a particular style or category of music
vocab.live performance: a concert or show where music is played in front of an audience
vocab.atmosphere: the feeling or mood in a place
vocab.talented: having a natural ability to do something well

phrases: On the one hand... on the other hand...

questions: What kind of music do you like?
Do you prefer listening to music alone or with friends?
Have you ever been to a live concert?
Do you play any musical instruments?
How do you usually discover new music?
answers: I would say I mostly listen to pop and indie music, but I also enjoy some electronic tracks when I am studying.
I prefer listening to music with friends, especially when we are driving or getting ready to go out. It makes the experience more fun.
Yes, I went to a small indie concert last year. The atmosphere was amazing because everyone knew the lyrics and sang along.
I used to play the guitar, but I stopped when school got busy. I would like to start again because it helps me relax.
I usually discover new music through streaming apps and recommendations from friends. Sometimes I also watch music videos on YouTube.

part2.question: Why do people enjoy going to live concerts?
part2.photoA: A huge crowd at an outdoor music festival with bright lights
part2.photoB: A small, intimate concert in a cosy club
part2.model: In photo A, there is a massive crowd at a festival, which shows the excitement of sharing music with thousands of people. In photo B, the concert is small and intimate, so the audience feels closer to the performer. I think people enjoy live concerts because the atmosphere is completely different from listening to a recording. There is something special about hearing music with other fans and feeling the energy in the room. Personally, I prefer smaller venues because you can see the artist better.

part3.task: A school wants to encourage more students to enjoy music. Discuss the ideas and decide which two would be most effective.
options: Inviting musicians to perform at school
A student battle of the bands
part3.model:
A: I think inviting musicians to perform would inspire students who are interested in music.
B: That's true, but it might only appeal to students who already like music.
A: I see what you mean. Music appreciation lessons could help everyone understand different genres.
B: Yes, but after-school music clubs give students a chance to actually play and create music.
A: So maybe music clubs and live performances at school would work best together.
B: Yes, I agree with you.

questions: Is live music better than recorded music?
Should music lessons be compulsory in schools?
How has streaming changed the music industry?
answers: I think live music is more emotional and exciting, but recorded music is more convenient because you can listen anytime. Both have their place.
I believe music lessons should be available to everyone, but not necessarily compulsory. Some students have no interest in music and should not be forced.
Streaming has made music more accessible, but artists earn less money per stream. It has also changed how people discover new songs.
A popular song usually has a catchy melody, relatable lyrics, and good production. Social media trends can also make a song famous very quickly.

homework.reading: Read an interview with a musician. Summarise their career path in three sentences.
homework.listening: Listen to a song in English and write down five words or phrases from the lyrics.
homework.writing: Write an essay (140–190 words): 'Is it better to listen to live music or recorded music?'
